title: 'jjf dep add' accepts cycles silently; cycled issues vanish from 'jjf ready'
body:
# Symptom

`jjf dep add` accepts edges that close dependency cycles. The
issues caught in the cycle become permanently invisible to
`jjf ready` (all are "blocked") with no diagnostic ever firing.

# Repro

```
$ A=$(jjf new --json -t A -F /dev/null | jq -r .id)
$ B=$(jjf new --json -t B -d $A -F /dev/null | jq -r .id)
$ C=$(jjf new --json -t C -d $B -F /dev/null | jq -r .id)
$ jjf dep add $A $C       # closes the cycle: A -> C -> B -> A
dep added: blocks A -> C
$ echo $?
0
$ jjf ready               # A, B, C all hidden — no warning
(no output)
```

Self-deps ARE blocked at write time (good). Multi-step cycles
aren't.

# Risk profile

With 10 projects × multiple agents × dep edges set both via
`jjf new -d` and `jjf dep add`, accidental cycles will happen.
The failure mode is silent (issues disappear from `ready`),
which is the worst kind for an automation system.

# Fix candidates

1. `jjf dep add` refuses to add an edge that would create a
   cycle (preflight DFS from the target back to the source).
2. `jjf ready` detects unreachable open issues caused by
   cycles and logs a warning (with the involved ids) on stderr.

Both are useful; (1) prevents, (2) recovers.

# Confidence

Reproducible 100%. Tested in `scratchpad/qa/r06-deps/`.
